title: He’s running Boston to support people like him with Type 1 diabetes
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Collin Bennett, 29, from New Braintree, is running the Boston Marathon for Team Beth Israel Lahey Health.
Diagnosed with Type 1 diabetes at 11, Bennett supports Joslin Diabetes Center for their impact on his life.
Bennett runs to inspire others with diabetes and honors his own journey overcoming its challenges.

title: Springfield native Ruth E. Carter keeps making Oscars history
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Ruth E. Carter secures her fifth Oscar nomination for 'Sinners,' becoming the most-nominated Black woman in Oscar history.
Carter was the first Black person to win Best Costume Design in 2019 and the first Black woman to win multiple Oscars in 2023.
Carter, originating from Springfield, draws on her Massachusetts roots, utilizing Brimfield's antique marketplaces for costume design.

title: Brookline schools would see more than 200 layoffs if override fails, officials say
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Brookline schools could face over 200 layoffs if voters reject a proposed property-tax override in May.
The district needs $18.5 million over three years to maintain current staffing and programs, officials report.
Failure to pass the override may lead to larger class sizes, course eliminations, and new after-school program fees.

title: What to know about the PETS Act aimed at protecting dogs and cats in Mass.
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: The PETS Act in Massachusetts aims to ban the sale of dogs and cats in pet shops to reduce puppy mill sourcing.
The bill would allow pet shops to partner with shelters for adoptions and address housing and insurance barriers for pet ownership.
Massachusetts Legislature is considering the PETS Act, which needs approval from both chambers before reaching the governor.

title: As snow melts, drought still a big issue for Mass.
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Massachusetts faces ongoing drought despite heavy February snowfall, with some regions elevated to Critical Drought status.
State officials encourage water conservation due to persistent low groundwater levels and below-average precipitation since 2024.
Massachusetts Water Resources Authority's system near Boston is not currently experiencing drought conditions.

title: Why some women are hiding their boyfriends online
author: Meredith Goldstein
bullets: Chanté Joseph's essay discusses women obscuring their boyfriends on Instagram to challenge traditional heterosexual norms.
Joseph argues that modern relationships are evolving, with women prioritizing independence over centering men in their public identity.
The essay sparked widespread debate, reflecting a shift in expectations around romantic partnerships and women's roles within them.

title: He’s running the Boston Marathon for a camp ‘where illness takes a back seat to fun’
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Griffin O'Neill is running the Boston Marathon to support The Hole in the Wall Gang Camp.
The camp provides a fun, medically safe environment for children with serious medical conditions, free of charge.
O'Neill's fiancée's sister, Reagan Carey, a former camper, is now a counselor after overcoming bone cancer.

title: Eagles reportedly ‘will not trade A.J. Brown at this time’ amid Patriots’ continued interest
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: The Eagles reportedly declined a trade offer for A.J. Brown from the Patriots, who offered first- and third-round picks.
Philadelphia may consider trading Brown after June 1 due to potential salary cap savings and lower dead cap hit.
The Patriots signed WR Romeo Doubs, potentially reducing the urgency to acquire Brown, despite continued interest.

title: Jayson Tatum set to return vs. Wizards after injury management off-day
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Jayson Tatum to play against the Washington Wizards after missing a game for Achilles injury management.
Tatum operates under a minutes restriction after returning from a right Achilles rupture 10 months ago.
Baylor Scheierman and Derrick White are probable, while Nikola Vucevic is out against the Wizards.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'War Machine' starring Alan Ritchson is now available for streaming on Netflix.
'Zootopia 2' featuring Jason Bateman and Ginnifer Goodwin is streaming on Disney+.
'Rooster' with Steve Carell is streaming on HBO Max, offering comedic relief and heart.
